name: Hoosier Harvest Apple Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. For the crust: In a bowl, combine flour, granulated sugar, and salt. Cut in butter flavor shortening using a pastry blender or two knives until all flour is blended into pea-sized chunks. Combine water, egg, and vinegar; pour into flour mixture. Mix lightly with a fork until dough forms a ball. Divide dough into half and press between hands to form two 5-6 inch pancakes. Wrap in plastic or aluminum foil and refrigerate.
3. For the bottom crust: Roll out dough on a floured surface into a circle, trimming 1 inch larger than an upside-down 9-inch pie plate. Loosen dough carefully, fold into quarters, unfold, and press into the pie plate. Trim edge even with the pie plate.
4. Prepare the filling: Combine brown sugar, granulated sugar, flour, and cinnamon. Cut in butter flavor shortening using a fork until crumbs form. Toss apples with crumb mixture and pour into the unbaked pie shell. Sprinkle cider over filling.
5. Roll the top crust the same as the bottom crust. Use a cookie cutter to make a cut-out in the center of the dough. Remove the cut-out, sprinkle lightly with sugar and cinnamon, and place on top of the filled pie. Press edges together. Cut even with the edge of the pie plate.
6. For glaze: Brush the top with milk and sprinkle with granulated sugar. Bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 30-40 minutes. Cover the edge of the pie with foil during the last 10 minutes, if necessary, to prevent over-browning. Cool until barely warm or to room temperature before serving.
7. Makes one 9-inch pie.
